title: Recover lost commits through the reflog
description: Use the reflog to find and restore commits that became unreachable after a reset, rebase, or branch deletion.
body: Use when commits seem lost after a hard reset, an aborted rebase, or a deleted branch. Do not assume the work is gone before checking the reflog. Steps: run git reflog to find the dangling commit's hash; inspect it with git show; create a branch at that hash or cherry-pick it back; verify the restored content. Validation signals include the recovered commit reachable from a new ref and its diff matching the lost work. Risk boundary: reflog entries expire with garbage collection, so recover promptly and avoid running aggressive gc while work is missing.
